briefing.text: Revision 69f1940cf50c of github.com/globulario/sensei. The authored architecture corpus documents 5 component(s), 5 boundary(ies), and 18 contract(s); 164 attention item(s) (failure modes and forbidden fixes) are tracked. Regions and flows have no authored source yet. Architecture-health scoring is not yet wired into this producer.

regions.name: Ungrouped
regions.responsibility: Components not yet assigned to an owner-authored architectural region. This is a placeholder grouping, not a claim about the system's real region structure.

limitations: synthetic placeholder: no owner-authored region entity exists yet for this repository (see availability.limitations)

components.name: AwarenessGraphService
components.responsibility: The read-only gRPC server. Compiles awareness into typed Query / Resolve / Metadata / Briefing / Preflight answers. It is the only legitimate read path into the graph for agents and the dashboard.

components.name: CorpusLoader
components.responsibility: The YAML→RDF importer. Walks docs/awareness, classifies each file by schema, and emits validated N-Triples. The single writer that turns authored YAML into the compiled graph; the seed is generated, never hand-edited.

components.name: DashboardArchitectureView
components.responsibility: The VS Code "Project Awareness" webview. Reads the graph over gRPC (Metadata / Query / Resolve / related) and renders counts, lists, and a local-neighborhood focus graph. Read-only: it must not mutate corpus or promote candidates.

components.name: GeneratedProtoBindings
components.responsibility: Generated protobuf and gRPC bindings shared by the client, server, and MCP bridge. This is the typed wire contract boundary compiled from proto/awareness_graph.proto.

components.name: OxigraphStore
components.responsibility: The RDF triple store backing the graph. Holds bytes; it is not the semantic authority — the service that writes and interprets the triples is. Read via the service, never bypassed.

boundaries.name: Candidate promotion boundary
boundaries.summary: Promoting a candidate into the active corpus is a guarded CLI step with human review. No automatic or UI-driven promotion.

boundaries.name: Corpus CLI mutation boundary
boundaries.summary: All corpus mutation flows through the awg CLI (rebuild / promote), never through the UI or a direct store write. The owner-legal write path.

boundaries.name: Dashboard read-only gRPC boundary
boundaries.summary: The dashboard may read Metadata / Query / Resolve / Briefing over gRPC, but it must not mutate the graph. Candidate promotion stays CLI/file guarded until a safe mutation API exists.

boundaries.name: Domain scope boundary
boundaries.summary: A knowledge node belongs to exactly one domain so truth never crosses repos accidentally. A query scoped to one domain returns only that domain's nodes plus shared meta-principles.

boundaries.name: Generated seed boundary
boundaries.summary: embeddata/awareness.nt is a generated artifact, regenerated by awg rebuild and never hand-edited. Editing it directly bypasses the YAML source of truth and the validation pipeline.

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph
contracts.summary: gRPC service AwarenessGraph with 15 RPC(s): Briefing, Impact, Query, Resolve, Metadata, Preflight, EditCheck, Propose, ReferenceSites, GetArchitectureControlSnapshot, ListArchitectureArtifacts, GetArchitectureArtifactState, GetOntologyNavigationDescriptor, PrepareArchitectAnswerDisposition, RecordArchitectAnswerDisposition.

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.Briefing
contracts.summary: Briefing composes a ~500-token prose briefing for editing a file or tackling a task. The single entry point agents should call before significant code changes. Composed from the same SPARQL queries that back Impact; the difference is presentation, not data. — rpc Briefing(BriefingRequest) returns (BriefingResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.EditCheck
contracts.summary: EditCheck evaluates a proposed edit's content against the active repo-scoped advisory rules ("detect" blocks) that apply to the file, in the caller's resolved domain. It is WARNING-ONLY: it never blocks, never modifies code, and returns warnings naming the rule a bad-shape edit would violate (e.g. replacing dispenser.Errf with fmt.Errorf in a Caddy Caddyfile unmarshaler). Domain scoping is identical to Briefing/Impact: a multi-domain graph with no domain fails closed; cross-domain rules never apply. See golang/server/scope.go. — rpc EditCheck(EditCheckRequest) returns (EditCheckResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.GetArchitectureArtifactState
contracts.summary: GetArchitectureArtifactState returns architecture.artifact_state/v1 for ONE exact node IRI. The canonical class is resolved by the controlstate registry from observed graph classes — never supplied by the caller. — rpc GetArchitectureArtifactState(GetArchitectureArtifactStateRequest) returns (GetArchitectureArtifactStateResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.GetArchitectureControlSnapshot
contracts.summary: GetArchitectureControlSnapshot returns architecture.control_snapshot/v1 — the bounded repository overview (typed counts, attention, authority). — rpc GetArchitectureControlSnapshot(GetArchitectureControlSnapshotRequest) returns (GetArchitectureControlSnapshotResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.GetOntologyNavigationDescriptor
contracts.summary: GetOntologyNavigationDescriptor returns ontology.navigation_descriptor/v1 — registry-derived, repository-independent; requires no filesystem context. — rpc GetOntologyNavigationDescriptor(GetOntologyNavigationDescriptorRequest) returns (GetOntologyNavigationDescriptorResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.Impact
contracts.summary: Impact returns the structured data the Briefing composer uses. Direct (file is the explicit subject of the node) vs Inferred (reached via package/symbol walks) partition matches the existing services-repo analysis package contract. — rpc Impact(ImpactRequest) returns (ImpactResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.ListArchitectureArtifacts
contracts.summary: ListArchitectureArtifacts returns one page of architecture.artifact_index/v1. The cursor is OPAQUE: produced and validated only by the controlstate owner, bound to snapshot/registry/filter identity, rejected on mismatch. — rpc ListArchitectureArtifacts(ListArchitectureArtifactsRequest) returns (ListArchitectureArtifactsResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.Metadata
contracts.summary: Metadata returns coverage and freshness signals about the graph itself — not about any specific file. Agents call this at session start so they can tell whether an EMPTY briefing means "no rules apply here" (graph is well-covered) vs "this area is unannotated" (graph coverage is thin in this domain).  Cheap: counts are computed live via SPARQL but the queries are bounded and small. Safe to call once per session or per task. — rpc Metadata(MetadataRequest) returns (MetadataResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.Preflight
contracts.summary: Preflight is the agent's pre-edit decision-support call. Given a task and optional file list, it returns a single risk_class the agent can branch on, plus required_actions, forbidden_fixes, tests_to_run, and files_to_read assembled from anchored graph facts (never invented).  Reuses Briefing's matching engine; adds risk classification, coverage discipline, and action-oriented response shape on top. EMPTY does NOT become LOW_RISK unless coverage explicitly says coverage is sufficient — if the graph doesn't know the area, the response is UNKNOWN_IMPACT and the agent is told to proceed carefully.  Store unavailable degrades to PREFLIGHT_STATUS_DEGRADED with UNKNOWN_IMPACT and blind_spots="awareness_store_unavailable" rather than hard-failing. Only returns codes.Unavailable if the handler cannot construct any response at all. — rpc Preflight(PreflightRequest) returns (PreflightResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.PrepareArchitectAnswerDisposition
contracts.summary: ── Phase 9.5 Checkpoint 5: the guarded architect-answer mutation family ── The FIRST and ONLY mutation RPCs in the control-panel surface. Each is a guarded delegation to an existing owner (questiondisposition / questionpromotion); the server assigns no authority. A refused mutation is a SUCCESSFUL response carrying a typed refusal (mirrors Propose REJECTED), never a transport error. Requests carry LOGICAL identities + an actor identity only — filesystem roots come from the startup-owned context. — rpc PrepareArchitectAnswerDisposition(PrepareArchitectAnswerDispositionRequest) returns (PrepareArchitectAnswerDispositionResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.Propose
contracts.summary: Propose submits a typed feedback entry (failure_mode, invariant, required_test, forbidden_fix, or contract_unknown) to the awareness corpus's review queue. It is the agent write path: an agent that learned something durable proposes it here.  The entry is validated with the SAME contract-first rules as `awg propose` and written as a candidate under docs/awareness/candidates/proposals/ — it is NOT a live graph node and does NOT trigger a rebuild. Promotion into the canonical corpus stays a human/CI-gated step, so the served graph never silently mutates durable truth (preserving the CLI-not-durable-mutation contract; the RPC only queues a proposal). Requires the server to be configured with a candidates directory; returns Unavailable otherwise. — rpc Propose(ProposeRequest) returns (ProposeResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.Query
contracts.summary: Query exposes constrained typed graph browsing only. ArchitectureClaim, OpenQuestion, and ArchitectAnswer are available here only when explicitly requested; they remain non-authoritative and are not surfaced through Briefing, Impact, or Preflight. — rpc Query(QueryRequest) returns (QueryResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed
generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.ReferenceSites
contracts.summary: ReferenceSites returns, for each requested code-symbol id, the set of OTHER code symbols that reference it (inbound aw:references edges from ingested SCIP occurrences). This is the completeness primitive: given the symbols a change touches, it answers "N sites share this reference; which ones did the diff miss?". Directed and complete — it reads inbound edges straight from the store, so a target with many OUTGOING references does not crowd out its referencing sites (as a bidirectional related-walk would). External targets (stdlib/third-party, id "external:<name>") have no in-repo definition and are ignored. Cheap: one inbound describe per id. — rpc ReferenceSites(ReferenceSitesRequest) returns (ReferenceSitesResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: AwarenessGraph.Resolve
contracts.summary: Resolve fetches a single node by its annotation ID (e.g. "invariant:foo", "pat.bar"). Returns the node's triples plus any code-anchor provenance. This is the tool agents call after reading a // @invariant: X comment, or when explicitly inspecting a non-authoritative architecture_claim, open_question, or architect_answer. — rpc Resolve(ResolveRequest) returns (ResolveResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

contracts.name: Candidate YAML corpus schema
contracts.summary: The authored-YAML schema (invariants, failure_modes, components, boundaries, …) the importer recognises. The single authoring substrate.

contracts.name: yaml2nt N-Triples output contract
contracts.summary: The importer emits N-Triples that are grammar-validated before write; an invalid stream is refused, never persisted.

attention.title: add a second package-identity authority
attention.summary: Introduce a second surface that defines which packages exist or their identity (kind/version/build_id) — a generated manifest, installer mirror, or tarball name — alongside packages/registry.yaml.

attention.title: consume the generated workspace as release authority
attention.summary: Consume services/generated as the authoritative release source instead of a disposable derived workspace.

attention.title: delete generated/ mid-flow without regenerating
attention.summary: Delete services/generated mid-flow without regenerating it, leaving the build to consume stale or missing inputs.

attention.title: Run sensei audit --fix to resolve embeddata-freshness in this repo
attention.summary: In a checkout with a sibling ../services repo present, 'sensei audit --fix' resolves svcRepo to that sibling and its embeddata-freshness fix path regenerates the COMBINED (services+awareness-graph) seed, then writes it straight over golang/server/embeddata/awareness.nt — inflating the file by ~174k lines and leaking internal services-repo content into what must stay a self-only public artifact. Discovered while closing issue #95: after correctly rebuilding the self-only seed via scripts/build-awareness-graph-self.sh, running 'sensei audit --fix' to clear a residual 13-triple STALE reading silently overwrote it with the combined regeneration. Always use scripts/build-awareness-graph-self.sh (optionally --check first) to refresh this repo's committed seed; never run 'sensei audit --fix' for embeddata-freshness when a sibling services checkout is present.

attention.title: Auto-promote or auto-complete after a successful disposition
attention.summary: Recording an accepted answer must never trigger governed promotion; a promotion must never trigger task completion or certification. Each lifecycle step is a separate, explicitly-authorized guarded action.

attention.title: Carry architectural state in color alone
attention.summary: A badge/row/tag whose meaning is conveyed only by color fails proof 22. Every state must also carry a text label or glyph.

attention.title: Classify rigor by filename, let a declaration downgrade an A/B surface, or exempt Class D from integrity gates
attention.summary: Assigning a rigor class to a raw filename (instead of a governed surface), letting a declared C/D class downgrade a change that touches an A/B-owned surface, or exempting Class D from the repository-integrity gates all defeat proportional rigor's purpose: they let ceremony land away from where the structural weight is, or let a light declaration bypass integrity. Effective rigor must be the strictest touched surface, fail closed to Class A on the unknown, and keep every integrity gate at Class D.

attention.title: Compose an incompleteness explanation or coverage percentage in the UI or from a missing denominator
attention.summary: Composing the known/missing/next-evidence wording in the webview (a client decision table), or rendering a coverage percentage without an observed denominator (or an observed-zero population as 0%/100%), launders absence into apparent knowledge and re-opens the collapse the Phase-9 laws forbid. The panel must render owner-projected strings verbatim and emit a percentage only for an observed denominator > 0.

attention.title: Copy raw architect answer text into a governed record or promotion prose
attention.summary: The raw answer is opaque bytes hashed by the owner (AnswerID + digest). Governed prose is an independently-authored ProposeRequest; copying answer text into it launders unverified prose into authority (Phase 9.6 provenance != authority).

attention.title: Expose the unguarded questiongen dialogue op as an authoritative mutation
attention.summary: questiongen RecordAnswer/AdjudicateAnswer carry no identity, authority, or ledger. Only questiondisposition/questionpromotion are guarded; exposing the dialogue layer as authority bypasses actor binding and the ledger.

attention.title: First-row-win over contested evidence receipts
attention.summary: Silently accepting the first receipt when a later receipt observes the same owner-path subject with a different payload. Same-subject/different-payload must be CONTESTED with both preserved; exact-digest is REPLAYED.

attention.title: First-row-wins on conflicting runtime evidence
attention.summary: Taking the first observation's classification when the same crossing is observed with conflicting evidence. Conflicting evidence must be preserved (all conflicting ids recorded) and yield contradictory_observations/degraded, never a silent satisfied.

attention.title: Gate CP6 closure on a flaky VS Code host-launch harness
attention.summary: Adding @vscode/test-electron (real host download + xvfb) to the closure path introduces cross-platform flake that can block a green Ubuntu+Windows gate. Packaged validation must stay a deterministic pure-Node content assertion over the .vsix.

attention.title: Hardcode ontology class membership capabilities or families in the editor
attention.summary: Navigation families, class membership, capabilities, and default visibility come only from the ontology navigation descriptor. A client-side ASPECTS/CLASS_COLOR/capability/family table drifts from the owner registry.

attention.title: Infer question-state next-action closure or lifecycle in the inspector
attention.summary: The inspector must render artifact_state fields verbatim; questions/evidence/next-action are owner ids with no state, and closure/lifecycle/severity come from the owner. Any client inference fabricates architectural truth.

attention.title: Invent a caller, callee, or contract from a probe observation
attention.summary: Populating RuntimeObservation caller/callee/EndpointOrContract with fabricated identities so an owner-path probe becomes an admissible crossing. Probe data has none of these; the adapter must leave the crossing identity unresolved and let the owner refuse it.

attention.title: Invent a promotion owner to satisfy proof 13
attention.summary: Adding a server-side promotion RPC or any fact-writing path to display 'promoted knowledge' violates graph_truth_must_come_from_approved_corpus and the briefing-feedback ownership boundary. Proof 13 is met by DISPLAYING existing Phase-9.6 ScopedFeedbackRef lineage; promotion stays deferred and visibly unavailable.

attention.title: Let a narrow width force horizontal scroll or hide critical attention
attention.summary: Collapsing the panel by horizontally scrolling the body, or hiding the rail/inspector/attention list at narrow widths, breaks proof 23. The narrow layout must stack into a single vertically-scrolling column with nothing hidden.

attention.title: Let caller input select a boundary's canonical class
attention.summary: Trusting a caller-supplied canonical class instead of resolving it from observed rdf:type. A fabricated Boundary class must never make an arbitrary node runtime-assessable; the class is recomputed and disagreement is refused.

attention.title: Map a probe result to a satisfied crossing verdict
attention.summary: Any path where evidence-level probe data yields satisfied/violated on a boundary crossing. Composed through the runtimeboundary owner, an honest probe observation must resolve to unknown/required_evidence_absent, never a positive verdict.

attention.title: Map an absent optional count to zero
attention.summary: proto3 optional counts decode to undefined when unobserved; rendering absence as 0 (e.g. Number(x||0)) launders unknown state into a false healthy zero. Presence must be read via the synthetic-oneof marker.

attention.title: Mark a degraded, stale, or unavailable probe as available/fresh
attention.summary: Reporting Availability=available or Freshness=fresh (or IntegrityVerified=true) for a probe that was inconclusive/failed/unavailable/stale/incomplete. Availability, freshness, and integrity must be mapped honestly from the probe result.

attention.title: Mint or authorize a boundary from observed traffic
attention.summary: Letting a runtime observation create a boundary, or admitting an observation without an explicit non-self-authorizing binding (in scope, target-matched), lets traffic author architecture. Observations are admissible only through a validated binding; unbound traffic is refused.

attention.title: Mutate without the expected-ledger-head precondition or present a refusal as mutation success
attention.summary: A commit must CAS against the client's expected ledger head (stale -> refuse, write nothing) and never silently re-prepare; a domain refusal must expose mutation_applied=false with the unchanged ledger identity, never a transport success that looks applied.

attention.title: Re-derive graph authority or severity in the webview
attention.summary: The CP3 control panel must read authority (observed/current/integrity) and severity from the control snapshot verbatim. Re-deriving them client-side (the pre-CP3 graphAuthority.ts/dashboard.js cockpit derivation) reintroduces the drift the migration removed.

attention.title: Recompute the runtime-boundary verdict in controlstate or the server
attention.summary: Re-deriving a runtime-boundary verdict downstream (importing runtimeboundary from controlstate/server, or reimplementing crossing classification / verdict / severity so the control panel decides compliance instead of projecting the owner's decision) spawns a semantic doppelganger: two owners disagree and telemetry can launder itself into architecture. The only admissible path is the runtimeboundarycompose bridge projecting the already-decided verdict verbatim; controlstate carries a typed DimensionObservation and the server carries it through the generic transport.

attention.title: Render a non-applicable dimension as open
attention.summary: Only owner-marked applicable dimensions are shown; a non-applicable dimension must never appear or count as open (missing non-applicable dimensions do not open an artifact).

attention.title: Render completion or authoritative_completion as correctness
attention.summary: Showing the top-strip completion state (or the 'authoritative' qualifier) as a correctness/certification claim conflates completion with correctness (proof 4). Phase 6 alone certifies; the panel must word completion as a workflow terminal state, never truth.

attention.title: Reuse legacy graph-adjacency as relationships in the control panel
attention.summary: No owner projection carries relationship/edge data. Relationships and the focus graph must render honestly unavailable, never fall back to legacy graph adjacency / CLASS_COLOR / DIR (which stay quarantined in dashboard.js).

attention.title: Treat missing or absent telemetry as satisfied
attention.summary: Returning satisfied (or any compliant verdict) when required runtime evidence is absent or the collector is unavailable. Missing telemetry must yield unavailable/unknown, never satisfied — and collector outage must stay distinct from no-violation-observed.

attention.title: Trust producer-declared freshness or trust on an evidence receipt
attention.summary: Never accept a receipt's self-declared freshness/trust/status as authoritative to reach PASS. The validator must recompute effective status against the proof request and binding. Producer-supplied freshness with no observed time is UNKNOWN, not PASS.

attention.title: Use ambient admission to judge a caller-supplied diff
attention.summary: awareness_audit_diff evaluates only the caller-supplied diff as a read-only, non-authoritative projection. It must not invoke repository admission or inspect ambient working-tree mutations, because those represent a different change subject and can produce a verdict unrelated to the submitted evidence.

attention.title: Yield satisfied under stale or unverified graph authority
attention.summary: Concluding satisfied when the boundary identity is not current or not integrity-verified. Stale/unverified authority means the boundary definition itself is untrusted, so no observation can be admitted as authoritative — the verdict is at best degraded.

attention.title: infer package identity from a binary or tarball
attention.summary: Derive a package's name/kind/version/build_id by inspecting a built binary or .tgz filename instead of reading packages/registry.yaml.

attention.title: keep undeclared binary aliases in packages/bin
attention.summary: Leave binaries in packages/bin that are not declared by a registry-backed canonical spec (stale or alias binaries).

attention.title: treat an adoption.Receipt or a graph node alone as authoritative promotion
attention.summary: Accept an adoption.Receipt (candidate evidence) or the mere existence of a governed graph node as proof that a reusable answer was authorized and correctly promoted, instead of a QuestionPromotionReceipt binding the full question -> answer -> authority -> result -> governed mutation -> rebuilt graph lineage.

attention.title: use an arbitrary actor string or implicitly reuse another authority for disposition/promotion/completion
attention.summary: Derive the answering actor, promotion actor, or completing actor from an arbitrary caller string, or silently reuse the certifying actor, instead of an explicit authority-bound actor verified via authority.VerifyActorBinding — with answer authority and promotion authority as separate typed operations.

attention.title: implement a bespoke RDF interpretation for the provenance query
attention.summary: Implement the offline provenance reader with a second, divergent RDF interpretation (IRI parsing, edge direction, literal handling) instead of reusing or matching store.Store semantics with parity tests.

attention.title: let completion answer, promote, rebuild, re-certify, or otherwise repair the loop it verifies
attention.summary: Have the completion transaction record a disposition, promote an answer, mutate governed sources, rebuild the graph, or re-run certification to make a task completable, instead of verify-only fail-closed behavior over the separate question/promotion owner and the certification receipt.

attention.title: convert a deferred or dismissed question into resolved without an authorized disposition
attention.summary: Reclassify a deferred/dismissed question as resolved (or drop it from the resolution summary) without a new authority-verified disposition, instead of keeping deferred/dismissed questions durable and explainable.

attention.title: copy a task-local answer into governed sources or a repository briefing
attention.summary: Promote or surface a task_local disposition into docs/awareness governed sources or a repository-wide/foreign-domain briefing, instead of keeping it confined to its exact task/session/result and effective scope.

attention.title: delete or drop an architect question to improve closure
attention.summary: Remove, hide, or drop a closure-blocking architect question (or rely on a rebuild that removes its backing blocker) to make proving/certification/completion pass, instead of an explicit authorized disposition preserved as lineage.

attention.title: write the graph or briefing projection directly during disposition/promotion/completion
attention.summary: Have a Phase-8 package write the awareness graph, seed, or a briefing/ledger projection directly instead of mutating the governed source and rebuilding through the deterministic owner path (and rebuilding ledger projections from the verified chain).

attention.title: overwrite an existing governed record to force a promotion
attention.summary: Overwrite or edit-in-place an existing canonical governed record whose semantics differ, to make a promotion succeed, instead of raising a typed collision/contradiction.

attention.title: call a CLI handler from a production package or duplicate CLI promotion policy
attention.summary: Have a Phase-8 production package invoke a cmd/awg run* handler (e.g. runPropose) or re-implement propose/rebuild policy inline, instead of a reusable governed-promotion owner sitting beneath sensei propose that both the CLI and Phase 8 call.

attention.title: model promotion journal states as task lifecycle events
attention.summary: Add the four promotion states to closureprotocol task LedgerEventTypes, project them into task phase/status, or let task-ledger reducers consume them, instead of a promotion-local typed vocabulary and validator.

attention.title: promote by writing or partial-regenerating the combined embedded seed
attention.summary: Have the promotion path edit, partial-regenerate, or claim convergence of golang/server/embeddata/awareness.nt (the cross-repository combined seed) instead of persisting only the repository-scoped .sensei/project/graph.nt and reporting the combined-seed obligation separately.

attention.title: release the promotion lock after source commit but before graph verification
attention.summary: Release the repository-scoped promotion/mutation lock after governed_source_committed and before governed_graph_verified, allowing a concurrent writer to change docs/awareness before the rebuild re-reads it from disk.

attention.title: recover a failed promotion by deleting the committed source mutation
attention.summary: Use rollback-by-deletion of a durably committed governed-source mutation as the recovery strategy, instead of resuming and reconciling the same content-addressed attempt from its journal.

attention.title: report a temporary graph compile as completed promotion
attention.summary: Treat an in-memory or throwaway graph compile as graph_verified, instead of requiring the canonical persisted .sensei/project/graph.nt plus authority marker to be independently reloaded and verified against the committed manifest.

attention.title: trust a caller-supplied governed status, graph digest, node IRI, or promotion status
attention.summary: Accept a caller-supplied governed=true, graph digest, node IRI, source digest, provenance verdict, or promotion status as authoritative instead of recomputing every trusted value from the committed source and reloaded graph.

attention.title: accept an artifact class from the client
attention.summary: Add a request field (or reuse an index filter) that lets the caller supply the canonical class used to select an artifact assessment policy.

attention.title: convert missing optional counts to zero
attention.summary: Fill an unobserved controlstate count/summary with an explicit zero on the wire (or drop proto3 optional and let absent decode as 0) so the response looks complete.

attention.title: derive repository identity from cwd or workspace folder
attention.summary: Establish repository root/domain from the current working directory, a workspace folder guess, or homeDomain, instead of the immutable startup-owned Phase 9.6 repository context.

attention.title: duplicate closure or severity tables in the server
attention.summary: Copy the controlstate closure, severity, lifecycle, or applicability vocabularies/mappings into the server, mapper, or client so a transport layer can classify state itself.

attention.title: expose raw internal errors or absolute repository paths
attention.summary: Serialize raw internal error strings, stack details, or absolute filesystem/repository paths into a control-panel response or gRPC status message.

attention.title: hardcode semantic class membership or capabilities in the editor
attention.summary: Carry ontology class membership, families, capabilities, or semantic visibility in the VS Code client instead of consuming the registry-derived navigation descriptor.

attention.title: infer an active lifecycle from absent lifecycle evidence
attention.summary: Report lifecycle active when no authoritative lifecycle source was observed.

attention.title: infer closure (or a satisfied dimension) from graph edge counts
attention.summary: Declare a dimension satisfied or an artifact closed from graph-edge presence/adjacency alone, without an explicit typed owner or verification making the evidence admissible.

attention.title: infer not_applicable from a missing assessment policy
attention.summary: Mark an artifact or dimension not_applicable because no reviewed policy exists, instead of unknown/unsupported.

attention.title: parse error text (or a label/color) to assign attention severity
attention.summary: Derive an attention class or severity from error strings, labels, or colors, or map severity client-side, or silently downgrade a critical source condition.

attention.title: parse reason prose to choose a protobuf enum
attention.summary: Inspect a reason code or human-readable detail string in the mapper/handler to decide which protobuf enum value (availability, closure, severity) to emit.

attention.title: reconstruct controlstate models from labels or colors
attention.summary: Rebuild closure/severity/availability state client- or server-side from display labels, colors, prose, or protobuf string fields instead of consuming the typed canonical projection.

attention.title: replace unknown/unavailable state with a friendly closed or zero state
attention.summary: Render unknown, unsupported, or unavailable state as closed, not_applicable, or zero to make the panel look healthier.

attention.title: treat partial or unavailable projections as empty success objects
attention.summary: Return an empty/zeroed response object (or a transport error) for a semantically partial/unavailable projection instead of the typed projection carrying availability, sources, and reasons.

attention.title: use closure.Report.Verdict as artifact closure
attention.summary: Copy a task/scope closure verdict or its dimension list onto an arbitrary graph artifact, or invent a repository-wide feedback scan from graph adjacency.

attention.title: add a filesystem repository root (or repo selector) to BriefingRequest
attention.summary: Add a repository-root or filesystem-path field to BriefingRequest, or otherwise let a remote caller select which repository's promotions the server verifies.

attention.title: erase the base graph briefing when feedback is degraded or unavailable
attention.summary: Drop or blank the existing graph/intent/implementation-pattern briefing, or upgrade combined status to OK, when feedback is degraded, unavailable, or invalid — instead of preserving the base briefing and composing status by the frozen table.

attention.title: infer an active task (or task directory) for task-only server feedback
attention.summary: For a task-only server briefing, manufacture a task binding — call a tasksession active-task fallback, read the only task directory, or emit a fabricated task id/session — instead of returning typed feedback_unavailable (canonical_task_scope_not_established).

attention.title: infer promotion scope by normalization/fallback, leak task-local answers, let debris poison scope, or move the feedback owner into server/editor
attention.summary: Admit a promotion by trimming/case-folding/basename/prefix/suffix/domain-or-file fallback, treat graph adjacency / claimed scope / issuer / status as authority, let an unrelated broken promotion degrade the requested briefing, render raw answer text as reusable governed knowledge, treat a feedback outage as proof no promoted knowledge exists, or let the future server/editor become the feedback owner.

attention.title: parse questionpromotion error strings or reimplement/duplicate promotion verification for briefing feedback
attention.summary: Classify a promotion verification outcome by parsing its error message, re-read promotion journals/receipts/governed-source/graph in briefingfeedback to reconstruct verification, or reimplement committed-promotion verification / scope / relevance / classification in the server or task briefing instead of the one canonical owner.

attention.title: manually construct an authoritative-looking feedback projection in the server
attention.summary: Hand-build a briefingfeedback.Projection (records or availability) in the server instead of obtaining it from briefingfeedback.Build or the narrow canonical unavailable-projection constructor, or map an unvalidated projection to the wire.

attention.title: use homeDomain or the requested domain as proof of filesystem repository identity
attention.summary: Treat homeDomain, the requested domain, cwd, a basename, or the first checkout as proof of which filesystem repository to verify, or verify promotions for a request whose exact resolved graph domain is not the configured repository domain.

attention.title: normalize or infer change-to-task identity instead of exact typed matching
attention.summary: Trim, case-fold, shorten, path-clean, or basename/prefix-match any change-to-task identity value, or infer the task from a branch name, commit message, --task-dir, or ambient Git state, or accept a stale-head or cross-repository binding, or resolve a contradictory publication set by selecting a record.

attention.title: express a producer failure as a 9.4b runtime degradation or promote local Git state to authoritative
attention.summary: Route a change-to-task producer failure (unsupported event, checkout mismatch, absent task/completion, unverifiable provenance, contradictory publication, write failure) through projection_owner_runtime_error or any 9.4b degraded-pass reason, or treat local/ambient Git execution as GitHub-authoritative, or publish a non-authoritative/partially-valid binding.

attention.title: enforce completion without first validating an authoritative change binding, or let an invalid binding degrade
attention.summary: Invoke the completion projection/owner (the 9.4b runtime lane) before an authoritative change-to-task binding is accepted, accept a binding on its self-described fields instead of exact current-subject correspondence, select one binding from a contradictory set, or let an absent/malformed/stale/mismatched/unverifiable binding reach a degraded pass.

attention.title: enforce the completion gate without per-domain opt-in
attention.summary: Make the completion gate block merges on a repo/domain that has not explicitly adopted enforce via its per-repo gate policy.

attention.title: fail the completion gate closed when Sensei is unavailable
attention.summary: Turn a Sensei/store/owner outage — where the closure verdict cannot be computed — into a blocking gate failure instead of a degraded (fail-open) pass.

attention.title: treat missing required task identity as runtime unavailability
attention.summary: Under enforce, classify an absent, ambiguous, malformed, or mismatched required task binding as "unavailable" and return a degraded pass, instead of failing closed as cannot-verify.

attention.title: invent a GNN or ML capability without repository evidence
attention.summary: Introduce a graph-neural-network / embedding-similarity / ML memory capability that has no grounding in repository evidence.

attention.title: reinterpret or re-derive completion truth inside a surface
attention.summary: Re-derive readiness, correctness, question resolution, or terminal cardinality in a CLI/MCP/server/CI/editor surface instead of reading the owner's reconstruction.

attention.title: ship a Phase-9 surface without a reviewed slice
attention.summary: Implement or enable a CLI, MCP, CI/GitHub, or editor surface without an open, reviewed Phase-9 slice carrying acceptance criteria, failure modes, and a proof matrix.

attention.title: a Phase-9 surface appends a completed event or writes a completion receipt
attention.summary: Let a CLI/MCP/server/CI/editor surface append a completed ledger event or produce/replace a completion receipt instead of delegating to the completion owner.

attention.title: treat a projection or the Phase-8 closure report as completion authority
attention.summary: Use a task-status view, CI gate projection, editor cockpit, or the Phase-8 closure report as if it were terminal completion authority or a task-completion fact.

attention.title: restore dist/bin as a long-lived authority path
attention.summary: Restore or depend on services/dist/bin as a long-lived authoritative binary path.

attention.title: revive a legacy spec forest in consumed paths
attention.summary: Reintroduce legacy or duplicate spec trees in consumed paths as an active authority.

attention.title: treat services/dist as a persistent source tree
attention.summary: Edit services/dist in place, or depend on its contents surviving across builds, instead of treating it as disposable release output. Observed this session: running the installer against dist/ in-place created root-owned files that blocked the next build's wipe.

attention.title: treat generated specs as the canonical recipe
attention.summary: Treat services/generated/specs (or any generated spec copy) as the canonical install recipe.

evolution.summary: first observed revision for this producer; no prior authoritative projection exists to compare against

focus_records.name: Ungrouped
focus_records.responsibility: Components not yet assigned to an owner-authored architectural region.

limitations: synthetic placeholder: no owner-authored region entity exists yet for this repository (see availability.limitations)

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraphService
focus_records.responsibility: The read-only gRPC server. Compiles awareness into typed Query / Resolve / Metadata / Briefing / Preflight answers. It is the only legitimate read path into the graph for agents and the dashboard.

focus_records.name: CorpusLoader
focus_records.responsibility: The YAML→RDF importer. Walks docs/awareness, classifies each file by schema, and emits validated N-Triples. The single writer that turns authored YAML into the compiled graph; the seed is generated, never hand-edited.

focus_records.name: DashboardArchitectureView
focus_records.responsibility: The VS Code "Project Awareness" webview. Reads the graph over gRPC (Metadata / Query / Resolve / related) and renders counts, lists, and a local-neighborhood focus graph. Read-only: it must not mutate corpus or promote candidates.

focus_records.name: GeneratedProtoBindings
focus_records.responsibility: Generated protobuf and gRPC bindings shared by the client, server, and MCP bridge. This is the typed wire contract boundary compiled from proto/awareness_graph.proto.

focus_records.name: OxigraphStore
focus_records.responsibility: The RDF triple store backing the graph. Holds bytes; it is not the semantic authority — the service that writes and interprets the triples is. Read via the service, never bypassed.

focus_records.name: Candidate promotion boundary
focus_records.responsibility: Promoting a candidate into the active corpus is a guarded CLI step with human review. No automatic or UI-driven promotion.

focus_records.name: Corpus CLI mutation boundary
focus_records.responsibility: All corpus mutation flows through the awg CLI (rebuild / promote), never through the UI or a direct store write. The owner-legal write path.

focus_records.name: Dashboard read-only gRPC boundary
focus_records.responsibility: The dashboard may read Metadata / Query / Resolve / Briefing over gRPC, but it must not mutate the graph. Candidate promotion stays CLI/file guarded until a safe mutation API exists.

focus_records.name: Domain scope boundary
focus_records.responsibility: A knowledge node belongs to exactly one domain so truth never crosses repos accidentally. A query scoped to one domain returns only that domain's nodes plus shared meta-principles.

focus_records.name: Generated seed boundary
focus_records.responsibility: embeddata/awareness.nt is a generated artifact, regenerated by awg rebuild and never hand-edited. Editing it directly bypasses the YAML source of truth and the validation pipeline.

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph
focus_records.responsibility: gRPC service AwarenessGraph with 15 RPC(s): Briefing, Impact, Query, Resolve, Metadata, Preflight, EditCheck, Propose, ReferenceSites, GetArchitectureControlSnapshot, ListArchitectureArtifacts, GetArchitectureArtifactState, GetOntologyNavigationDescriptor, PrepareArchitectAnswerDisposition, RecordArchitectAnswerDisposition.

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.Briefing
focus_records.responsibility: Briefing composes a ~500-token prose briefing for editing a file or tackling a task. The single entry point agents should call before significant code changes. Composed from the same SPARQL queries that back Impact; the difference is presentation, not data. — rpc Briefing(BriefingRequest) returns (BriefingResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.EditCheck
focus_records.responsibility: EditCheck evaluates a proposed edit's content against the active repo-scoped advisory rules ("detect" blocks) that apply to the file, in the caller's resolved domain. It is WARNING-ONLY: it never blocks, never modifies code, and returns warnings naming the rule a bad-shape edit would violate (e.g. replacing dispenser.Errf with fmt.Errorf in a Caddy Caddyfile unmarshaler). Domain scoping is identical to Briefing/Impact: a multi-domain graph with no domain fails closed; cross-domain rules never apply. See golang/server/scope.go. — rpc EditCheck(EditCheckRequest) returns (EditCheckResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.GetArchitectureArtifactState
focus_records.responsibility: GetArchitectureArtifactState returns architecture.artifact_state/v1 for ONE exact node IRI. The canonical class is resolved by the controlstate registry from observed graph classes — never supplied by the caller. — rpc GetArchitectureArtifactState(GetArchitectureArtifactStateRequest) returns (GetArchitectureArtifactStateResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.GetArchitectureControlSnapshot
focus_records.responsibility: GetArchitectureControlSnapshot returns architecture.control_snapshot/v1 — the bounded repository overview (typed counts, attention, authority). — rpc GetArchitectureControlSnapshot(GetArchitectureControlSnapshotRequest) returns (GetArchitectureControlSnapshotResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.GetOntologyNavigationDescriptor
focus_records.responsibility: GetOntologyNavigationDescriptor returns ontology.navigation_descriptor/v1 — registry-derived, repository-independent; requires no filesystem context. — rpc GetOntologyNavigationDescriptor(GetOntologyNavigationDescriptorRequest) returns (GetOntologyNavigationDescriptorResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.Impact
focus_records.responsibility: Impact returns the structured data the Briefing composer uses. Direct (file is the explicit subject of the node) vs Inferred (reached via package/symbol walks) partition matches the existing services-repo analysis package contract. — rpc Impact(ImpactRequest) returns (ImpactResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.ListArchitectureArtifacts
focus_records.responsibility: ListArchitectureArtifacts returns one page of architecture.artifact_index/v1. The cursor is OPAQUE: produced and validated only by the controlstate owner, bound to snapshot/registry/filter identity, rejected on mismatch. — rpc ListArchitectureArtifacts(ListArchitectureArtifactsRequest) returns (ListArchitectureArtifactsResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.Metadata
focus_records.responsibility: Metadata returns coverage and freshness signals about the graph itself — not about any specific file. Agents call this at session start so they can tell whether an EMPTY briefing means "no rules apply here" (graph is well-covered) vs "this area is unannotated" (graph coverage is thin in this domain).  Cheap: counts are computed live via SPARQL but the queries are bounded and small. Safe to call once per session or per task. — rpc Metadata(MetadataRequest) returns (MetadataResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.Preflight
focus_records.responsibility: Preflight is the agent's pre-edit decision-support call. Given a task and optional file list, it returns a single risk_class the agent can branch on, plus required_actions, forbidden_fixes, tests_to_run, and files_to_read assembled from anchored graph facts (never invented).  Reuses Briefing's matching engine; adds risk classification, coverage discipline, and action-oriented response shape on top. EMPTY does NOT become LOW_RISK unless coverage explicitly says coverage is sufficient — if the graph doesn't know the area, the response is UNKNOWN_IMPACT and the agent is told to proceed carefully.  Store unavailable degrades to PREFLIGHT_STATUS_DEGRADED with UNKNOWN_IMPACT and blind_spots="awareness_store_unavailable" rather than hard-failing. Only returns codes.Unavailable if the handler cannot construct any response at all. — rpc Preflight(PreflightRequest) returns (PreflightResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.PrepareArchitectAnswerDisposition
focus_records.responsibility: ── Phase 9.5 Checkpoint 5: the guarded architect-answer mutation family ── The FIRST and ONLY mutation RPCs in the control-panel surface. Each is a guarded delegation to an existing owner (questiondisposition / questionpromotion); the server assigns no authority. A refused mutation is a SUCCESSFUL response carrying a typed refusal (mirrors Propose REJECTED), never a transport error. Requests carry LOGICAL identities + an actor identity only — filesystem roots come from the startup-owned context. — rpc PrepareArchitectAnswerDisposition(PrepareArchitectAnswerDispositionRequest) returns (PrepareArchitectAnswerDispositionResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.Propose
focus_records.responsibility: Propose submits a typed feedback entry (failure_mode, invariant, required_test, forbidden_fix, or contract_unknown) to the awareness corpus's review queue. It is the agent write path: an agent that learned something durable proposes it here.  The entry is validated with the SAME contract-first rules as `awg propose` and written as a candidate under docs/awareness/candidates/proposals/ — it is NOT a live graph node and does NOT trigger a rebuild. Promotion into the canonical corpus stays a human/CI-gated step, so the served graph never silently mutates durable truth (preserving the CLI-not-durable-mutation contract; the RPC only queues a proposal). Requires the server to be configured with a candidates directory; returns Unavailable otherwise. — rpc Propose(ProposeRequest) returns (ProposeResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.Query
focus_records.responsibility: Query exposes constrained typed graph browsing only. ArchitectureClaim, OpenQuestion, and ArchitectAnswer are available here only when explicitly requested; they remain non-authoritative and are not surfaced through Briefing, Impact, or Preflight. — rpc Query(QueryRequest) returns (QueryResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.RecordArchitectAnswerDisposition
focus_records.responsibility: rpc RecordArchitectAnswerDisposition(RecordArchitectAnswerDispositionRequest) returns (RecordArchitectAnswerDispositionResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.ReferenceSites
focus_records.responsibility: ReferenceSites returns, for each requested code-symbol id, the set of OTHER code symbols that reference it (inbound aw:references edges from ingested SCIP occurrences). This is the completeness primitive: given the symbols a change touches, it answers "N sites share this reference; which ones did the diff miss?". Directed and complete — it reads inbound edges straight from the store, so a target with many OUTGOING references does not crowd out its referencing sites (as a bidirectional related-walk would). External targets (stdlib/third-party, id "external:<name>") have no in-repo definition and are ignored. Cheap: one inbound describe per id. — rpc ReferenceSites(ReferenceSitesRequest) returns (ReferenceSitesResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: AwarenessGraph.Resolve
focus_records.responsibility: Resolve fetches a single node by its annotation ID (e.g. "invariant:foo", "pat.bar"). Returns the node's triples plus any code-anchor provenance. This is the tool agents call after reading a // @invariant: X comment, or when explicitly inspecting a non-authoritative architecture_claim, open_question, or architect_answer. — rpc Resolve(ResolveRequest) returns (ResolveResponse)

limitations: generator-derived from proto/awareness_graph.proto (assertion: inferred), not independently governed

focus_records.name: Candidate YAML corpus schema
focus_records.responsibility: The authored-YAML schema (invariants, failure_modes, components, boundaries, …) the importer recognises. The single authoring substrate.

focus_records.name: yaml2nt N-Triples output contract
focus_records.responsibility: The importer emits N-Triples that are grammar-validated before write; an invalid stream is refused, never persisted.
